     def get_subsidies_for_address(
diff --git a/cloud/subsidies/tests/test_interface.py b/cloud/subsidies/tests/test_interface.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..11d82747c6
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cloud/subsidies/tests/test_interface.py
@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
+
+from django.utils import timezone
+
+import pytest
+
+from cloud.location.enums import Country
+from cloud.planning.enums import MainHardwareType
+from cloud.subsidies.interface import SubsidyInterface
+
+if TYPE_CHECKING:
+    from cloud.subsidies.models import Subsidy
+
+
+def test_valid_subsidies__country(subsidy_no: Subsidy):
+    valid_subsidies = SubsidyInterface.valid_subsidies(country=Country.SE)
+    assert subsidy_no not in valid_subsidies.all()
+
+    valid_subsidies = SubsidyInterface.valid_subsidies(country=Country.NO)
+    assert subsidy_no in valid_subsidies.all()
+
+
+def test_valid_subsidies__valid_period(subsidy_no: Subsidy):
+    now = timezone.now()
+
+    # Subsidy is valid today
+    subsidy_no.valid_from = (now - timezone.timedelta(days=1)).date()
+    subsidy_no.valid_to = (now + timezone.timedelta(days=1)).date()
+    subsidy_no.save()
+    valid_subsidies = SubsidyInterface.valid_subsidies(country=Country.NO)
+    assert subsidy_no in valid_subsidies.all()
+
+    # Subsidy is expired
+    subsidy_no.valid_from = (now - timezone.timedelta(days=2)).date()
+    subsidy_no.valid_to = (now - timezone.timedelta(days=1)).date()
+    subsidy_no.save()
+    valid_subsidies = SubsidyInterface.valid_subsidies(country=Country.NO)
+    assert subsidy_no not in valid_subsidies.all()
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    'subsidy_applicable_for, hardware_types_filter, subsidy_should_be_included',
+    [
+        ([], [MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM, MainHardwareType.BATTERY], True),
+        ([MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM], [MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM], True),
+        ([MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM], [MainHardwareType.BATTERY], False),
+        ([MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM], [MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM, MainHardwareType.BATTERY], True),
+        ([MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM, MainHardwareType.BATTERY], [MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM], True),
+        (
+            [MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM, MainHardwareType.BATTERY],
+            [MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM, MainHardwareType.BATTERY],
+            True,
+        ),
+        ([MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM, MainHardwareType.BATTERY], [MainHardwareType.HEAT_PUMP], False),
+    ],
+)
+def test_valid_subsidies__filter_by_hardware_type(
+    subsidy_no: Subsidy, subsidy_applicable_for, hardware_types_filter, subsidy_should_be_included
+):
+    subsidy_no.applicable_for = subsidy_applicable_for
+    subsidy_no.save()
+
+    valid_subsidies = SubsidyInterface.valid_subsidies(
+        country=Country.NO, applicable_for=hardware_types_filter
+    )
+    if subsidy_should_be_included:
+        assert subsidy_no in valid_subsidies.all()
+    else:
+        assert subsidy_no not in valid_subsidies.all()

commit ce6818d3071d443cf6c9731c451df4361735919b
Author: Wojciech Mietlinski <votech@otovo.com>
Date:   Thu Jul 25 14:00:37 2024 +0200

    location: add main_hardware_type param to suggest endpoint
    
    What
    This commit adds a main_hardware_type param to the suggest endpoint.
    This param will be used to decide what kind suggestions to return. Right
    now if the main_hardware_type equals 'battery_system' then no filter
    will be applied to suggestions.
    
    Why
    We need this for upcoming "battery first" sales flow.

commit deeaedbc4253d0183019d9ab2125ec168c4a0d60
Author: Mathias Wold <45951843+mathiaswold@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Wed Jul 31 14:41:43 2024 +0200

    shoppingcart: Filter subsidies on main hardware types
    
    When creating standalone battery offers (and soon automatic
    calculations) we do not want to include subsidies that are not
    applicable for batteries. Subsidies already has an `applicable_for`
    field which now is used to filter out subsidies given a shopping cart's
    main hardware types.

@@ -193,6 +193,9 @@ class ShoppingCart(BusinessUnitMixin, TimestampsBase, models.Model):

diff --git a/cloud/shoppingcart/jinja2/shoppingcart/base_markups/list.html b/cloud/shoppingcart/jinja2/shoppingcart/base_markups/list.html
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..9cbb4f7a56
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cloud/shoppingcart/jinja2/shoppingcart/base_markups/list.html
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{% from "rainbow/forms.html" import render_field %}
+{% from "datetimes.html" import date, datetime, timesince %}
+{% from "macros.html" import admin_link, amount, user_icon %}
+
+{% macro header_cells(key) %}
+  <th>{{ _('Markup') }}</th>
+  <th>{{ _('Min') }}</th>
+  <th>{{ _('Max') }}</th>
+{% endmacro %}
+
+{% macro data_cells(rules, key) %}
+  {% if key in rules %}
+    <td>{{ rules[key].markup_percent }} %</td>
+    <td>{{ amount(rules[key].minimum_base_markup) }}</td>
+    <td>{{ amount(rules[key].maximum_base_markup) }}</td>
+  {% else %}
+    <td colspan="3"></td>
+  {% endif %}
+{% endmacro %}
+{% set batteries = 'battery_system' in enabled_hardware %}
+{% set evc = 'ev_charger_system' in enabled_hardware %}
+{% set other = 'other_system' in enabled_hardware %}
+
+<div class="_mt-12 _overflow-x-auto _w-full">
+  <h4>Previous markups</h4>
+  <table class="_table _table-large">
+    <thead>
+      <tr>
+        <th colspan="3"></th>
+        <th colspan="{{ 6 if batteries else 3 }}">{{ _('Standalone') }}</th>
+        {% if batteries %}<th colspan="6">{{ _('Bundled') }}</th>{% endif %}
+        {% if evc or other %}<th>{{ _('Add-ons') }}</th>{% endif %}
+      </tr>
+      <tr>
+        <th colspan="3"></th>
+        <th colspan="3">{{ _('PV') }}</th>
+        {% if batteries %}
+          <th colspan="3">{{ _('Battery') }}</th>
+          <th colspan="3">{{ _('PV') }}</th>
+          <th colspan="3">{{ _('Battery') }}</th>
+        {% endif %}
+        {% if evc %}<th colspan="3">{{ _('EVC') }}</th>{% endif %}
+        {% if other %}<th colspan="3">{{ _('Other') }}</th>{% endif %}
+      </tr>
+      <tr>
+        <th class="!_px-0"></th>
+        <th>{{ _('Created by') }}</th>
+        <th>{{ _('Active From') }}</th>
+        {{ header_cells() }}
+        {% if batteries %}
+          {{ header_cells() }}
+          {{ header_cells() }}
+          {{ header_cells() }}
+        {% endif %}
+        {% if evc %}{{ header_cells() }}{% endif %}
+        {% if other %}{{ header_cells() }}{% endif %}
+      </tr>
+    </thead>
+    <tbody>
+      {% for model in base_markups %}
+        {% set rules = model.get_all_rules_dict() %}
+
+        <tr>
+          <td class="!_px-0">{{ admin_link(request, model, extra_classes="_px-1") }}</td>
+          <td>{{ user_icon(model.created_by) }}</td>
+          <td>{{ datetime(model.active_period.lower) }}</td>
+          {{ data_cells(rules, 'rule_pv_system') }}
+          {% if batteries %}
+            {{ data_cells(rules, 'rule_battery_system') }}
+            {{ data_cells(rules, 'rule_pv_system_bundled') }}
+            {{ data_cells(rules, 'rule_battery_system_bundled') }}
+          {% endif %}
+          {% if evc %}{{ data_cells(rules, 'rule_ev_charger_system_bundled') }}{% endif %}
+          {% if other %}{{ data_cells(rules, 'rule_other_system_bundled') }}{% endif %}
+        </tr>
+      {% endfor %}
+    </tbody>
+  </table>
+</div>

+def _get_all_markups(business_unit: BusinessUnit) -> list[ShoppingCartBaseMarkup]:
+    base_markups = []
+    previous = None
+    for markup in (
+        ShoppingCartBaseMarkup.objects.filter(business_unit=business_unit)
+        .select_related('created_by')
+        .prefetch_related('rules')
+        .order_by('active_period')
+    ):
+        diff = markup.diff(previous)
+        # Check that the new markup is not a partner rule update
+        if diff and any(k.startswith('rule_') for k in diff):
+            base_markups.append(markup)
+        previous = markup
+    return base_markups[::-1]
+
+
+@staff_required
+@permission_required('shoppingcart.view_shoppingcartbasemarkup', raise_exception=True)
+def list_base_markups(request: OtovoRequest) -> HttpResponse:
+    business_unit_id = (request.GET or request.POST).get('business_unit')
+    business_unit = get_object_or_404(BusinessUnit.all_for_user(request.user), id=business_unit_id)
+    base_markups = _get_all_markups(business_unit)
+    enabled_hardware = business_unit.config.get_enabled_hardware()
+    context = {
+        'business_unit': business_unit,
+        'base_markups': base_markups,
+        'enabled_hardware': enabled_hardware,
+    }
+    return render(request, 'shoppingcart/base_markups/list.html', context)
+
+

     return separator.join(markets)
 
 
+def get_latest_main_hardware_types(
+    interest: Interest, existing_contact: ContactSimplePublicObject | None
+) -> str:
+    """
+    Provide a list of main hardware types that we assume the customer is interested in.
+
+    Non-activated interest: Return either PV or battery
+    ----------------------------------------------------
+    At this stage Cloud has calculated many hardware combinations and we do not yet know which of them the
+    customer is interested in. But we can distinguish the battery only sales flow from the classic PV-centered
+    sales flow based on the latest shopping cart from the calculation. To deliver the most precise information
+    available, we denote the sales flow in use by returning only one main hardware type, either PV or battery.
+
+    Activated interest: Return all selected hardware types
+    ------------------------------------------------------
+    After activation, we have an accepted shopping cart that tells us what particular hardware combination the
+    customer is interested in.
+    """
+    fallback_value = (
+        existing_contact.properties.get('latest_main_hardware_types', '') if existing_contact else ''
+    )
+    # Non-activated interest
+    if interest.activated_at is None:
+        if not (calculation := interest.active_calculation):
+            return fallback_value
+        if not (shopping_cart := calculation.shopping_carts.order_by('created_at').last()):
+            return fallback_value
+        return (
+            MainHardwareType.BATTERY.value
+            if shopping_cart.is_standalone_battery
+            else MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM.value
+        )
+    # Activated interest
+    else:
+        if not (calculation := interest.last_accepted_calculation):
+            return fallback_value
+        if not (shopping_cart_package := calculation.get_accepted_shopping_cart_package()):
+            return fallback_value
+        return ';'.join(
+            main_hardware_type.value
+            for main_hardware_type in shopping_cart_package.shopping_cart.main_hardware_types
+        )
+
+

     *,
     interest: Interest,
@@ -189,6 +234,9 @@ def _get_hubspot_contact_properties(

+        'latest_main_hardware_types': get_latest_main_hardware_types(
+            interest=interest, existing_contact=contact
+        ),

@@ -0,0 +1,165 @@
+import datetime
+
+import pytest
+from hubspot.crm.contacts import SimplePublicObject as ContactSimplePublicObject
+
+from cloud.hubspot.interface import get_latest_main_hardware_types
+from cloud.interests.tests.factories import CalculationFactory, InterestFactory, ShoppingCartPackageFactory
+from cloud.planning.enums import MainHardwareType
+from cloud.shoppingcart.tests.factories import ShoppingCartFactory
+from cloud.timelib.timezones import OSLO_TZ
+
+EXISTING_CONTACT = ContactSimplePublicObject(
+    properties={'latest_main_hardware_types': 'previously-synced-main-hardware-types'}
+)
+
+
+@pytest.mark.django_db
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    'existing_contact, expected_result',
+    [(None, ''), (EXISTING_CONTACT, 'previously-synced-main-hardware-types')],
+)
+def test__non_activated_interest__interest_has_no_active_calculation(
+    existing_contact, expected_result, mocker
+):
+    interest = InterestFactory.create(activated_at=None)
+    mock_active_calculation = mocker.patch(
+        'cloud.interests.models.Interest.active_calculation', new_callable=mocker.PropertyMock
+    )
+    mock_active_calculation.return_value = None
+
+    result = get_latest_main_hardware_types(interest=interest, existing_contact=existing_contact)
+
+    assert result == expected_result
+
+
+@pytest.mark.django_db
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    'existing_contact, expected_result',
+    [(None, ''), (EXISTING_CONTACT, 'previously-synced-main-hardware-types')],
+)
+def test__non_activated_interest__active_calculation_has_no_shopping_cart(existing_contact, expected_result):
+    interest = InterestFactory.create(activated_at=None)
+    calculation = CalculationFactory.create(interest=interest, automatic=True)
+
+    assert calculation.shopping_carts.count() == 0
+
+    result = get_latest_main_hardware_types(interest=interest, existing_contact=existing_contact)
+
+    assert result == expected_result
+
+
+@pytest.mark.django_db
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    'is_standalone_battery, expected_result',
+    [(True, MainHardwareType.BATTERY.value), (False, MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM.value)],
+)
+def test__non_activated_interest__success(
+    product_version_models, is_standalone_battery, expected_result, mocker
+):
+    interest = InterestFactory.create(activated_at=None)
+
+    timestamp_1 = datetime.datetime(2024, 7, 1, tzinfo=OSLO_TZ)
+    timestamp_2 = datetime.datetime(2024, 7, 2, tzinfo=OSLO_TZ)
+
+    shopping_cart_1 = ShoppingCartFactory.create(created_at=timestamp_1)
+    shopping_cart_2 = ShoppingCartFactory.create(created_at=timestamp_2)
+
+    def dummy_is_standalone_battery(self):
+        """
+        Verify that Cloud picks the latest shopping cart by providing the expected value in the latest
+        shopping cart and a different value in all other shopping carts.
+        """
+        if self.created_at == timestamp_2:
+            return is_standalone_battery
+        return not is_standalone_battery
+
+    mocker.patch(
+        'cloud.shoppingcart.models.ShoppingCart.is_standalone_battery',
+        new_callable=lambda: property(dummy_is_standalone_battery),
+    )
+
+    assert shopping_cart_1.is_standalone_battery == (not is_standalone_battery)
+    assert shopping_cart_2.is_standalone_battery == is_standalone_battery
+
+    calculation = CalculationFactory.create(interest=interest, automatic=True)
+    ShoppingCartPackageFactory.create(calculation=calculation, shopping_cart=shopping_cart_1)
+    ShoppingCartPackageFactory.create(calculation=calculation, shopping_cart=shopping_cart_2)
+
+    result = get_latest_main_hardware_types(interest=interest, existing_contact=None)
+
+    assert result == expected_result
+
+
+@pytest.mark.django_db
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    'existing_contact, expected_result',
+    [(None, ''), (EXISTING_CONTACT, 'previously-synced-main-hardware-types')],
+)
+def test__activated_interest__interest_has_no_last_accepted_calculation(
+    existing_contact, expected_result, mocker
+):
+    interest = InterestFactory.create(activated_at=datetime.datetime(2024, 7, 1, tzinfo=OSLO_TZ))
+    mock_last_accepted_calculation = mocker.patch(
+        'cloud.interests.models.Interest.last_accepted_calculation', new_callable=mocker.PropertyMock
+    )
+    mock_last_accepted_calculation.return_value = None
+
+    result = get_latest_main_hardware_types(interest=interest, existing_contact=existing_contact)
+
+    assert result == expected_result
+
+
+@pytest.mark.django_db
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    'existing_contact, expected_result',
+    [(None, ''), (EXISTING_CONTACT, 'previously-synced-main-hardware-types')],
+)
+def test__activated_interest__last_accepted_calculation_has_no_shopping_cart_package(
+    existing_contact, expected_result
+):
+    interest = InterestFactory.create(activated_at=datetime.datetime(2024, 7, 1, tzinfo=OSLO_TZ))
+    calculation = CalculationFactory.create(interest=interest, automatic=True)
+
+    assert calculation.shoppingcartpackage_set.count() == 0
+
+    result = get_latest_main_hardware_types(interest=interest, existing_contact=existing_contact)
+
+    assert result == expected_result
+
+
+@pytest.mark.django_db
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    'main_hardware_types, expected_result',
+    [
+        ([MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM], 'pv_system'),
+        (
+            [MainHardwareType.BATTERY, MainHardwareType.EV_CHARGER, MainHardwareType.HEAT_PUMP],
+            'battery_system;ev_charger_system;heat_pump_system',
+        ),
+    ],
+)
+def test__activated_interest__success(main_hardware_types, expected_result, mocker):
+    interest = InterestFactory.create(activated_at=datetime.datetime(2024, 7, 1, tzinfo=OSLO_TZ))
+    calculation = CalculationFactory.create(interest=interest)
+
+    mock_last_accepted_calculation = mocker.patch(
+        'cloud.interests.models.Interest.last_accepted_calculation', new_callable=mocker.PropertyMock
+    )
+    mock_last_accepted_calculation.return_value = calculation
+
+    shopping_cart = ShoppingCartFactory.create()
+    ShoppingCartPackageFactory.create(calculation=calculation, shopping_cart=shopping_cart, accepted=True)
+
+    mock_main_hardware_types = mocker.patch(
+        'cloud.shoppingcart.models.ShoppingCart.main_hardware_types', new_callable=mocker.PropertyMock
+    )
+    mock_main_hardware_types.return_value = main_hardware_types
+
+    assert (
+        interest.last_accepted_calculation.get_accepted_shopping_cart_package().shopping_cart == shopping_cart
+    )
+
+    result = get_latest_main_hardware_types(interest=interest, existing_contact=None)
+
+    assert result == expected_result

commit 81acb2ebc7c57d072cf7a24e31a949c8e2ee6026
Author: Mathias Wold <45951843+mathiaswold@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Wed Jul 31 09:30:23 2024 +0200

    interests: Use separate feature toggle for secondary system in offers
    
    The secondary system in offer creation feature was added in #12963. In
    that PR we used the "Other" node feature toggle to control whether the
    secondary system form should be shown in the shopping cart offer
    creation view. We now want to enable this in Norway separately from the
    "Other" node feature, so we add a new feature toggle for this.

     HEAT_PUMP_ENABLED_FOR_SALES = 'CLOUD-heat-pump-enabled-for-sales'
     SHOPPING_CART_OTHER_NODE = 'CLOUD-shopping-cart-other-node'
+    SECONDARY_SYSTEM_IN_OFFERS = 'CLOUD-secondary-system-in-offers'

+from cloud.hubspot.interface import get_markets
+from cloud.location.enums import Country
+
+
+def test__existing_contact_has_no_markets_set(contact):
+    assert not contact.properties.get('markets')
+    assert get_markets(market=Country.BE, contact=contact) == 'be'
+
+
+def test__existing_contact_has_one_market_set(contact):
+    contact.properties['markets'] = 'se'
+    assert get_markets(market=Country.BE, contact=contact) == 'se;be'
+
+
+def test__contact_has_several_markets_set(contact):
+    contact.properties['markets'] = 'se;no'
+    assert get_markets(market=Country.BE, contact=contact) == 'se;no;be'
+
+
+def test__no_existing_contact():
+    assert get_markets(market=Country.BE, contact=None) == 'be'

@@ -1,10 +1,12 @@
 from __future__ import annotations
 
+import logging
 from datetime import datetime, time
 from json import JSONDecodeError
 from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
 
 from cloud.financial.payments_module.exceptions.payments import PaymentsException
+from cloud.financial.salesorder.enums import Status
 from cloud.financial.salesorder.models import Order
 
 if TYPE_CHECKING:
@@ -12,6 +14,8 @@ if TYPE_CHECKING:
 
     from cloud.planning.models import Project
 
+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+
 
 def raise_response_error(response: Response) -> None:
     code = response.status_code
@@ -32,6 +36,7 @@ def get_installation_date(project: Project) -> datetime | None:
 
 
 def associate_invoices_with_orders(payments_response: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
+    # Order association determined by payments
     ids = [
         str(invoice['created_from'])
         for invoice in payments_response
@@ -41,6 +46,27 @@ def associate_invoices_with_orders(payments_response: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
     orders_dicted = {str(order.id): order for order in filtered_orders}
     for invoice in payments_response:
         invoice['created_from'] = orders_dicted.get(invoice['created_from'])
+
+    # Leftovers with no association, see if we can do it from cloud
+    for invoice in payments_response:
+        if not invoice['created_from']:
+            qs = Order.objects.filter(status=Status.SENT_TO_PAYMENTS)
+            if invoice.get('project_number', None):
+                qs = qs.filter(project__project_number=invoice['project_number'])
+            if invoice.get('customer', None):
+                qs = qs.filter(customer__customer_number=invoice['customer'])
+            # If we have a single order, we can associate it
+            if qs.count() == 1:
+                order = qs.first()
+                assert order
+                invoice['created_from'] = order
+                logger.info(
+                    'Associated invoice %s with order %s based on %s',
+                    invoice['invoice_id'],
+                    order.id,
+                    qs.explain(),
+                )
+
     return payments_response

diff --git a/cloud/financial/project/jinja2/project/invoices_list.html b/cloud/financial/project/jinja2/project/invoices_list.html
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..f168ef5591
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cloud/financial/project/jinja2/project/invoices_list.html
@@ -0,0 +1,114 @@
+{% from "rainbow/components/dropdownMenu/macros.html" import DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuItemContainer %}
+{% from "rainbow/components/dropdownButton/macros.html" import DropdownButton %}
+{% from "project/credit_invoice.html" import credit_invoice %}
+{% from "rainbow/components/slideOver/macros.html" import SlideOver %}
+{% from "project/repayment_plan.html" import RepaymentPlanSlideover %}
+{% from "project/credit_checks.html" import CreditChecksSlideover %}
+
+{% macro InvoicesList(project,customer, invoices, csrf_input) %}
+  <!--  Slideovers for the advanced button -->
+  {% if repayment_plans %}
+    {% set repayment_plan_slideover_id = "repayment-plan-slideover" %}
+
+    {{ RepaymentPlanSlideover(repayment_plans,project, repayment_plan_slideover_id) }}
+  {% endif %}
+  {% set credit_checks_slideover_id = "credit-checks-slideover" %}
+
+  {{ CreditChecksSlideover(credit_checks, project, credit_checks_slideover_id) }}
+  <div class="float-right">
+    <div class="_inline-flex">
+      <button type="submit"
+              class="_btn _btn-primary text-white hover:bg-transparent _ml-2">View Sales Orders</button>
+      {% call DropdownMenu() %}
+        <button type="submit"
+                class="_btn _btn-primary text-white hover:bg-transparent _ml-1 _dropdown-toggle-btn _w-8 _h-full">
+          <i class="fa-solid fa-chevron-down"></i>
+        </button>
+      </div>
+      <!-- Advanced button options.
+        Repayment Plan
+        Credit Checks etc. -->
+      {% call DropdownMenuItemContainer() %}
+        <div class="_bg-white _p-2 _w-64 position-absolute _right-4">
+          <button id="{{ repayment_plan_slideover_id }}"
+                  class="_dropdown-item _modal-show-trigger _text-lilac-100 _w-full _slide-over-show-trigger"
+                  {% if not repayment_plans %}disabled{% endif %}>{{ _("Repayment Plan") }}</button>
+          <button id="{{ credit_checks_slideover_id }}"
+                  class="_dropdown-item _modal-show-trigger _text-lilac-100 _w-full _slide-over-show-trigger">
+            {{ _("Credit Checks") }}
+          </button>
+        </div>
+      {% endcall %}
+    {% endcall %}
+  </div>
+  <!-- Main table with Invoices, only those where the customer is the paying party -->
+  <div id="project-customer-invoices-container" class="_w-3/4">
+    {{ vite_asset("cloud/rainbow/modules/rainbow/js/dropdown-menu.ts") }}
+    <table class="_table">
+      <thead>
+        <tr>
+          <th>Invoice Id</th>
+          <th>Created From</th>
+          <th>Created Date</th>
+          <th>Due Date</th>
+          <th>Status</th>
+          <th>Total Amount</th>
+          <th>Credited</th>
+          <th>Actions</th>
+        </tr>
+      </thead>
+      <tbody>
+        {% for invoice in invoices %}
+          {% set modal_credit_id = "confirm_crediting_"+  invoice.invoice_id| string %}
+
+          <!-- Crediting invoice -->
+          {{ credit_invoice(invoice, customer, request, modal_credit_id, csrf_input) }}
+          <tr>
+            <td>{{ invoice.invoice_id }}</td>
+            <td>
+              {% if invoice.created_from %}
+                <a href="{{ invoice.created_from.get_absolute_url() }}">{{ invoice.created_from.get_billing_schedule() }}</a>
+              {% else %}
+                {{ invoice.created_from }}
+              {% endif %}
+            </td>
+            <td>{{ invoice.created_date }}</td>
+            <td>{{ invoice.due_date }}</td>
+            <td>{{ _("Paid") if invoice.paid else invoice.payment_status }}</td>
+            <td>{{ invoice.total_amount }}</td>
+            <td>{{ invoice.credited }}</td>
+            <td>
+              {% call DropdownMenu() %}
+                <button class="_dropdown-toggle-btn">
+                  <i class="fa-solid fa-ellipsis _ml-2"></i>
+                </button>
+                {% call DropdownMenuItemContainer() %}
+                  <div class="_bg-white _p-2 _w-64 position-absolute _right-4">
+                    {% if invoice.pdf_id %}
+                      <a href="{{ payments_external_url }}/file/{{ invoice.pdf_id }}?download=true"
+                         class="_dropdown-item _text-lilac-100"
+                         download>{{ _('View PDF') }}</a>
+                    {% else %}
+                      <!-- No pdf to download, greyed out text -->
+                      <a class="_dropdown-item _text-grey-20" style="pointer-events: none">{{ _('View PDF') }}</a>
+                    {% endif %}
+                    {% if invoice.created_from %}
+                      <a href="{{ invoice.created_from.get_absolute_url() }}"
+                         class="_dropdown-item _text-lilac-100">{{ _('Go to Order') }}</a>
+                    {% else %}
+                      <a class="_dropdown-item _text-grey-20" style="pointer-events: none">{{ _('Go to Order') }}</a>
+                    {% endif %}
+                    <a id="{{ modal_credit_id }}"
+                       class="_dropdown-item _modal-show-trigger _text-lilac-100 _w-full">
+                      {{ _("Credit invoice") }}
+                    </a>
+                  </div>
+                {% endcall %}
+              {% endcall %}
+            </td>
+          </tr>
+        {% endfor %}
+      </tbody>
+    </table>
+  </div>
+{% endmacro %}

commit 41d6686760d77e981a210adc93110daae89eda01
Author: Mathias Wold <45951843+mathiaswold@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Tue Jul 30 12:34:56 2024 +0200

    shoppingcart: Add has_other_hardware property
    
    Instead of always saying that a cart has the Other main hardware type,
    we now check if the constructed product tree would place nodes in the
    Other group in any purchase model.

diff --git a/cloud/shoppingcart/models.py b/cloud/shoppingcart/models.py
index 1a083f84f8..d5b26307d5 100644
--- a/cloud/shoppingcart/models.py
+++ b/cloud/shoppingcart/models.py
@@ -325,6 +325,29 @@ class ShoppingCart(BusinessUnitMixin, TimestampsBase, models.Model):
     def is_standalone_battery(self) -> bool:
         return self.has_batteries and not self.has_pv_system
 
+    @property
+    def has_other_hardware(self) -> bool:
+        """True if the product tree would place nodes in the Other group in any purchase model."""
+        purchase_models = (
+            [self.project.purchase_model] if self.project else self.business_unit.config.purchase_models
+        )
+        for purchase_model in purchase_models:
+            # Construct a tree only with the structure needed to query where hardware is.
+            # We cannot apply markup here as that would result in a recursive loop.
+            tree = (
+                ProductTree(self)
+                .with_product_hierarchy()
+                .with_product_override()
+                .with_cost_items(main_purchase_model=purchase_model)
+                .with_extra_items()
+            )
+            other_hardware = tree.get_node(ProductCategory.OTHER_HARDWARE)
+            other_service = tree.get_node(ProductCategory.OTHER_SERVICE)
+            if len(other_hardware.children) > 0 or len(other_service.children) > 0:
+                return True
+
+        return False
+
     @property
     def main_hardware_types(self) -> list[MainHardwareType]:
         main_hardware_types = []
@@ -336,7 +359,7 @@ class ShoppingCart(BusinessUnitMixin, TimestampsBase, models.Model):
             main_hardware_types.append(MainHardwareType.EV_CHARGER)
         if self.has_heat_pumps:
             main_hardware_types.append(MainHardwareType.HEAT_PUMP)
-        if self.business_unit.config.shopping_cart_other_node_enabled:
+        if self.has_other_hardware:
             main_hardware_types.append(MainHardwareType.OTHER)
         return main_hardware_types
 
@@ -1263,10 +1286,6 @@ class ShoppingCartBaseMarkup(BusinessUnitMixin, TimestampsBase, models.Model):
             for rule in selected_rules
         }
 
-        # No need to enforce rule for Other hardware type
-        if MainHardwareType.OTHER not in rules:
-            all_system_hardware.discard(MainHardwareType.OTHER)
-

             set(rules.keys()) == all_system_hardware
         ), f'missing rules on markup {self.id}, expected {all_system_hardware} got {set(rules.keys())}'

    lease.

    want to lease.

commit 311b021a01ae5cc9a7cad7ea07d3ae9c4d8b633d
Author: Mathias Wold <45951843+mathiaswold@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Fri Jul 26 12:47:21 2024 +0200

    interests: Add support for secondary system in offer creation page
    
    This is a temporary solution to allow pricing of
    inverters/batteries/smart meters that are not a direct part of the
    primary system. For now this will be used in the UK to sell AC coupled
    inverters from Solax. This commit only touches the sales side of this
    feature.
    
    In the future we hope to refactor the concept of buildings into
    "systems", where each system can have one or more surfaces and their
    own requirements. This will require a larger refactor of the offer
    creation page as well as the edit spec/surfaces pages.
    
    Slack thread: https://otovo.slack.com/archives/C04EM31AVHB/p1721904750851749

commit a095fd511d761957fca20c78c2b579721a80852e
Author: Fredrik Gyllenhammar <fredrikgy@otovo.com>
Date:   Sat Jul 27 11:01:21 2024 +0200

    Add `primary_building` property to Spec
    
    This is a temporary measure while we get things sorted with Multi-System
    support. Allowing fro buildings to have their own requirements means
    that many properties on the Spec start loosing their meaning because we
    no longer have one shared inverter requiremnet accross all buildings,
    But we want to roll this out without rewriting all dependant codepaths,
    this delegates these Spec level properties to the first building, while
    we slowly remove them.

+            system_power = current_shopping_cart.old_spec.get_system_power()
+            building_age = current_shopping_cart.old_spec.get_building_age_in_years()
+        elif price_spec := project.price_spec:
+            spec: OldSpec = price_spec.get_installation_spec()
+            system_power = (spec.get_system_power(),)
+            building_age = (spec.get_building_age_in_years(),)
+            location_is_farm_or_b2b = (spec.location_is_farm_or_b2b(),)
+
+        else:
+            raise ValueError("I don't know if this should happen")
+        return building_age, location_is_farm_or_b2b, system_power
+
     @staticmethod

diff --git a/cloud/shoppingcart/dataclasses.py b/cloud/shoppingcart/dataclasses.py
index e60aaca93c..0bba6549d6 100644
--- a/cloud/shoppingcart/dataclasses.py
+++ b/cloud/shoppingcart/dataclasses.py
@@ -16,6 +16,7 @@ if TYPE_CHECKING:
     from typing_extensions import Self
 
     from cloud.shoppingcart.models import CartModification
+    from cloud.shoppingcart.product_tree.enums import ProductCategory
     from cloud.shoppingcart.product_tree.hierarchy.node import ProductNode
 
 
@@ -151,11 +152,13 @@ class NodeFinancingData:
     purchase_model: ClassVar[PurchaseModel]
     node: InitVar[ProductNode]
 
+    product_category: ProductCategory = field(init=False)
     total_gross_price: Money = field(init=False)
     total_gross_price_unmodified: Money = field(init=False)
     discounts: list[CartModification] = field(init=False)
 
     def __post_init__(self, node: ProductNode):
+        self.product_category = node.product_category
         self.total_gross_price = node.total_gross_price
         self.total_gross_price_unmodified = node.total_gross_price_unmodified
         self.discounts = node.get_all_modifications(include_free_months=True)

 DecimalField = partial(
     serializers.DecimalField, decimal_places=0, max_digits=10, rounding=decimal.ROUND_HALF_UP
@@ -20,6 +20,7 @@ DecimalField = partial(
 
 class BaseNodeFinancingDataSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
     purchase_model_code = serializers.CharField(source='purchase_model.value')
+    product_category = serializers.CharField(source='product_category.value')
     total_gross_price = DecimalField(source='total_gross_price.amount')
     total_gross_price_unmodified = DecimalField(source='total_gross_price_unmodified.amount')
     discounts = serializers.SerializerMethodField()
@@ -42,6 +43,11 @@ class ShoppingCartFinancingOptionSerializer(BaseNodeFinancingDataSerializer):
     pv_data = GroupFinancingDataSerializer()
     battery_data = GroupFinancingDataSerializer()
     ev_charger_data = GroupFinancingDataSerializer()
+    # NOTE:
+    # We are most likely transitioning to having the above group financing data as a list of groups
+    # instead of having them as separate fields. This is to make it easier to work with the data
+    # in Web. For now, we are keeping the old fields as well.
+    groups = serializers.SerializerMethodField()
 
     # Leasing specific fields
     leasing_annual_percentage_rate = serializers.DecimalField(required=False, decimal_places=2, max_digits=4)
@@ -73,3 +79,7 @@ class ShoppingCartFinancingOptionSerializer(BaseNodeFinancingDataSerializer):
     total_net_price_including_loan_costs = DecimalField(
         required=False, source='total_net_price_including_loan_costs.amount'
     )
+
+    def get_groups(self, obj: ShoppingCartFinancingOption):
+        non_null_groups = list(filter(None, [obj.pv_data, obj.battery_data, obj.ev_charger_data]))
+        return GroupFinancingDataSerializer(non_null_groups, many=True).data

commit f3f345fae88e15e6e9e9965fafa8b820ef84ee00
Author: Mathias Wold <45951843+mathiaswold@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Wed Jul 24 10:39:29 2024 +0200

    interests: Add available package options API
    
    This commit adds an API endpoint to the interest API that returns
    the available package options for the interest.
    
    This will be used by the client to determine which
    packages are available for the interest, and which packages can be
    updated via the update_package endpoint.

                 package_size=serializer.validated_data['package_size'],

             )
-        # TODO Handle PricePackageGenerateError
+        except PricePackageGenerateError:
+            return Response('No package available for this configuration', status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
         except Exception as exc:
             logger.error(exc)
             return Response(status=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)
@@ -374,6 +380,32 @@ class InterestAPIViewSet(viewsets.GenericViewSet, mixins.CreateModelMixin, mixin
             data=ShoppingCartPackageSerializer(package, context={'use_new_financing_options': True}).data
         )
 
+    @decorators.action(detail=True, methods=['GET'])
+    def package_options(self, request: Request, **kwargs):
+        """
+        Returns the available package options for the interest, which is a list of
+        valid package option combinations we should be able to generate a price for.
+        This should be used by the client to determine which packages are available
+        to fetch via the above update_package method.
+
+        The purpose of this endpoint is that it is faster than finding the cheapest
+        price for each package option. It also enables us to lazily create shopping
+        carts and related data when/if the user selects a package, instead of creating
+        all of them upfront on initial interest creation.
+        """
+        interest = self.get_object()
+
+        match request.query_params.get('system_type'):
+            case MainHardwareType.PV_SYSTEM:
+                package_options = get_available_pv_package_options(interest=interest)
+            case MainHardwareType.BATTERY:
+                package_options = get_available_battery_package_options(interest=interest)
+            case _:
+                return Response('Invalid system type', status=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)
+
+        serializer = PackageOptionSerializer(package_options, many=True)
+        return Response(serializer.data)
+
     @decorators.action(detail=True, methods=['post'])
     def activate(self, request, pk=None):
         interest = self.get_object()
diff --git a/cloud/interests/package_options.py b/cloud/interests/package_options.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..3feb8bed03
--- /dev/null
+++ b/cloud/interests/package_options.py
@@ -0,0 +1,132 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import logging
+from dataclasses import dataclass
+from itertools import product
+from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
+
+from cloud.costengine.equipment_combinations import get_all_hardware_combinations
+from cloud.costengine.exceptions import EquipmentSelectionError
+from cloud.costengine.interface import get_effective_cost_models
+from cloud.costengine.selection import find_available_models
+from cloud.featuretoggles import featuretoggles
+from cloud.featuretoggles.enums import FeatureToggleName
+from cloud.interests.automatic_specs import get_default_battery_spec_builder
+from cloud.planning.enums import MainHardwareType
+from cloud.solarspecs.enums import ProductClassification
+
+if TYPE_CHECKING:
+    from cloud.costengine.dataclasses import EffectiveCostModel
+    from cloud.interests.enums import PackageSize
+    from cloud.interests.models import Interest
+    from cloud.solarspecs.spec import Spec as OldSpec
+
+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
+
+
+@dataclass(kw_only=True, frozen=True)
+class InterestPackageOption:
+    system_type: MainHardwareType
+    product_classification: ProductClassification
+    package_size: PackageSize
+
+    # battery specific
+    with_battery: bool
+    minimum_battery_capacity: int | None = None
+
+    # ev charger specific
+    with_ev_charger: bool
+
+
+def _cost_model_has_hardware_combination(spec: OldSpec, effective_cost_model: EffectiveCostModel):
+    try:
+        return bool(
+            get_all_hardware_combinations(
+                address=spec.address,
+                buildings=spec.buildings,
+                cost_model=effective_cost_model,
+                return_first_combination=True,
+            )
+        )
+    except EquipmentSelectionError:
+        return False
+
+
+def get_available_pv_package_options(interest: Interest) -> list[InterestPackageOption]:
+    raise NotImplementedError()
+
+
+def get_available_battery_package_options(interest: Interest) -> list[InterestPackageOption]:
+    """
+    Creates a list of available battery package options for the given interest.
+    Battery sizes considered, based on the business config mapping:
+    - small
+    - economic
+    - large
+
+    Hardware combinations considered:
+    - Battery + Inverter
+    - Battery + Inverter + EV Charger
+    """
+
+    business_unit = interest.business_unit
+    config = business_unit.config
+
+    if not (
+        business_unit.config.batteries_in_auto_calc
+        and featuretoggles.is_enabled(
+            FeatureToggleName.STANDALONE_BATTERY_AUTO_CALCS, country=business_unit.market
+        )
+    ):
+        logger.warning(
+            'Tried to get available battery package options, but standalone battery calculations are not enabled in %s',
+            business_unit,
+        )
+        return []
+
+    applicable_cost_models = find_available_models(
+        address=interest.address,
+        sale_at=interest.price_at,
+    ).filter(costmodelbattery__isnull=False, eligible_for_standalone_battery_installations=True)
+
+    effective_cost_models = get_effective_cost_models(
+        cost_models=applicable_cost_models,
+        price_at=interest.price_at,
+    ).values()
+
+    package_size_to_minimum_capacity: dict[PackageSize, int] = config.battery_package_size_to_minimum_capacity
+
+    possible_package_combinations = product(
+        package_size_to_minimum_capacity.keys(),
+        [False, True] if config.ev_chargers_in_auto_calc else [False],
+    )
+
+    available_package_options: list[InterestPackageOption] = []
+
+    for package_size, with_ev_charger in possible_package_combinations:
+        minimum_capacity = package_size_to_minimum_capacity[package_size]
+
+        spec_builder = get_default_battery_spec_builder(
+            address=interest.address,
+            business_unit=interest.business_unit,
+            minimum_capacity=minimum_capacity,
+            with_ev_charger=with_ev_charger,
+        )
+
+        old_spec = spec_builder.build_old_spec()
+
+        for effective_cost_model in effective_cost_models:
+            if _cost_model_has_hardware_combination(old_spec, effective_cost_model):
+                available_package_options.append(
+                    InterestPackageOption(
+                        system_type=MainHardwareType.BATTERY,
+                        product_classification=ProductClassification.STANDARD,
+                        package_size=package_size,
+                        with_battery=True,
+                        minimum_battery_capacity=minimum_capacity,
+                        with_ev_charger=with_ev_charger,
+                    )
+                )
+                break
+
+    return available_package_options
diff --git a/cloud/interests/serializers.py b/cloud/interests/serializers.py
index a38216c28d..69d141eb42 100644
--- a/cloud/interests/serializers.py
+++ b/cloud/interests/serializers.py
@@ -956,10 +956,33 @@ class SolarPlannerConfigSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
     )
 
 
-class InterestUpdateCalculationSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
-    package_size = serializers.CharField()
+class PackageOptionSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
+    system_type = serializers.CharField()
+    # NOTE: Web refers to thr product classification as "product_name". This might change in the future.
+    product_name = serializers.CharField(
+        source='product_classification', required=False, default=ProductClassification.STANDARD
+    )
+    package_size = serializers.CharField(required=False, default=PackageSize.ECONOMIC)
+
+    # battery specific
+    # with_battery = serializers.BooleanField(required=False, default=False) # TODO will be used when PV is supported
+    minimum_battery_capacity = serializers.IntegerField(required=False)
+
+    # ev charger specific
     with_ev_charger = serializers.BooleanField(required=False, default=False)
 
+    def validate_system_type(self, system_type):
+        try:
+            return MainHardwareType(system_type)
+        except ValueError as e:
+            raise serializers.ValidationError('Invalid system type') from e
+
+    def validate_product_classification(self, product_classification):
+        try:
+            return ProductClassification(product_classification)
+        except ValueError as e:
+            raise serializers.ValidationError('Invalid product classification') from e
+
     def validate_package_size(self, package_size):
         try:
             return PackageSize(package_size)
diff --git a/cloud/interests/tests/test_package_options.py b/cloud/interests/tests/test_package_options.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..63c173ca84

+++ b/cloud/interests/tests/test_package_options.py
@@ -0,0 +1,194 @@
+import pytest
+
+from cloud.costengine.tests.conftest import *
+from cloud.featuretoggles.enums import FeatureToggleName
+from cloud.interests.enums import PackageSize
+from cloud.interests.package_options import InterestPackageOption, get_available_battery_package_options
+from cloud.planning.enums import MainHardwareType
+from cloud.solarspecs.enums import ProductClassification
+
+
+@pytest.fixture
+def mocked_battery_config(market_mock, mock_feature_toggle):
+    market_mock('batteries_in_auto_calc', return_value=True)
+    market_mock(
+        'battery_package_size_to_minimum_capacity',
+        return_value={
+            PackageSize.SMALL: 3000,
+            PackageSize.ECONOMIC: 7000,
+            PackageSize.LARGE: 15000,
+        },
+    )
+    market_mock('battery_package_default_system_power', return_value=10000)
+    mock_feature_toggle(FeatureToggleName.STANDALONE_BATTERY_AUTO_CALCS, is_enabled=True)
+
+
+@pytest.fixture
+def battery_cost_model(cost_model_no, hybrid_inverter, battery_inverter):
+    """
+    Cost model with batteries and hybrid/battery inverters, where the
+    smart meters and batteries are compatible with the inverters.
+    """
+    cost_model_no.eligible_for_standalone_battery_installations = True
+    cost_model_no.save()
+    CostModelInverter.objects.create(
+        cost_model=cost_model_no,
+        inverter_model_id=hybrid_inverter.id,
+        cost=20000,
+    )
+    CostModelInverter.objects.create(
+        cost_model=cost_model_no,
+        inverter_model_id=battery_inverter.id,
+        cost=10000,
+    )
+    for model in list(cost_model_no.smart_meters.all()) + list(cost_model_no.batteries.all()):
+        model.compatible_inverters.add(hybrid_inverter)
+        model.compatible_inverters.add(battery_inverter)
+    return cost_model_no
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize('ev_chargers_in_auto_calc', [False, True])
+def test_get_available_battery_package_options__all_available(
+    market_mock, mocked_battery_config, interest, battery_cost_model, ev_chargers_in_auto_calc
+):
+    market_mock('ev_chargers_in_auto_calc', return_value=ev_chargers_in_auto_calc)
+
+    non_evc_package_options = {
+        InterestPackageOption(
+            system_type=MainHardwareType.BATTERY,
+            product_classification=ProductClassification.STANDARD,
+            package_size=PackageSize.SMALL,
+            with_battery=True,
+            minimum_battery_capacity=3000,
+            with_ev_charger=False,
+        ),
+        InterestPackageOption(
+            system_type=MainHardwareType.BATTERY,
+            product_classification=ProductClassification.STANDARD,
+            package_size=PackageSize.ECONOMIC,
+            with_battery=True,
+            minimum_battery_capacity=7000,
+            with_ev_charger=False,
+        ),
+        InterestPackageOption(
+            system_type=MainHardwareType.BATTERY,
+            product_classification=ProductClassification.STANDARD,
+            package_size=PackageSize.LARGE,
+            with_battery=True,
+            minimum_battery_capacity=15000,
+            with_ev_charger=False,
+        ),
+    }
+
+    evc_package_options = {
+        InterestPackageOption(
+            system_type=MainHardwareType.BATTERY,
+            product_classification=ProductClassification.STANDARD,
+            package_size=PackageSize.SMALL,
+            with_battery=True,
+            minimum_battery_capacity=3000,
+            with_ev_charger=True,
+        ),
+        InterestPackageOption(
+            system_type=MainHardwareType.BATTERY,
+            product_classification=ProductClassification.STANDARD,
+            package_size=PackageSize.ECONOMIC,
+            with_battery=True,
+            minimum_battery_capacity=7000,
+            with_ev_charger=True,
+        ),
+        InterestPackageOption(
+            system_type=MainHardwareType.BATTERY,
+            product_classification=ProductClassification.STANDARD,
+            package_size=PackageSize.LARGE,
+            with_battery=True,
+            minimum_battery_capacity=15000,
+            with_ev_charger=True,
+        ),
+    }
+
+    expected_package_options = non_evc_package_options
+    if ev_chargers_in_auto_calc:
+        expected_package_options |= evc_package_options
+
+    assert set(get_available_battery_package_options(interest)) == expected_package_options
+
+
+def test_get_available_battery_package_options__missing_one_package_size(
+    market_mock, mocked_battery_config, interest, battery_cost_model
+):
+    # We have no batteries large enough for the large package size
+    market_mock(
+        'battery_package_size_to_minimum_capacity',
+        return_value={
+            PackageSize.SMALL: 3000,
+            PackageSize.ECONOMIC: 7000,
+            PackageSize.LARGE: 100000,
+        },
+    )
+
+    package_options = get_available_battery_package_options(interest)
+
+    # EVC not enabled, and missing the large package size
+    assert len(package_options) == 2
+    assert all(not option.with_ev_charger for option in package_options)
+    assert package_options[0].package_size == PackageSize.SMALL
+    assert package_options[0].minimum_battery_capacity == 3000
+    assert package_options[1].package_size == PackageSize.ECONOMIC
+    assert package_options[1].minimum_battery_capacity == 7000
+
+
+def test_get_interest_calculation_config_api__battery_system(
+    api_client, market_mock, mocked_battery_config, interest, battery_cost_model
+):
+    market_mock('ev_chargers_in_auto_calc', return_value=True)
+    response = api_client.get(
+        f'/web/interests/{interest.id}/package_options/',
+        data={'system_type': str(MainHardwareType.BATTERY)},
+    )
+    assert response.status_code == 200
+
+    assert response.data == [
+        {
+            'system_type': 'battery_system',
+            'product_name': 'standard',
+            'package_size': 'small',
+            'minimum_battery_capacity': 3000,
+            'with_ev_charger': False,
+        },
+        {
+            'system_type': 'battery_system',
+            'product_name': 'standard',
+            'package_size': 'small',
+            'minimum_battery_capacity': 3000,
+            'with_ev_charger': True,
+        },
+        {
+            'system_type': 'battery_system',
+            'product_name': 'standard',
+            'package_size': 'economic',
+            'minimum_battery_capacity': 7000,
+            'with_ev_charger': False,
+        },
+        {
+            'system_type': 'battery_system',
+            'product_name': 'standard',
+            'package_size': 'economic',
+            'minimum_battery_capacity': 7000,
+            'with_ev_charger': True,
+        },
+        {
+            'system_type': 'battery_system',
+            'product_name': 'standard',
+            'package_size': 'large',
+            'minimum_battery_capacity': 15000,
+            'with_ev_charger': False,
+        },
+        {
+            'system_type': 'battery_system',
+            'product_name': 'standard',
+            'package_size': 'large',
+            'minimum_battery_capacity': 15000,
+            'with_ev_charger': True,
+        },
+    ]

commit 459308db01019c29f2f262776aae19238dfa3580
Author: Anna Irene Andresen <69513935+annaiandresen@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Mon Jul 29 00:21:44 2024 +0200

    installerapp/scheduling: Small fixes (#12971)
    
    This PR includes several small changes to the calendar.
    
    1. We now push the query param to the url. You can now navigate back and forth if you have picked an installer, and you can access the calendar directly by id if needed.
    
    2. Updates the installer name in the calendar header again after I removed it in https://github.com/otovo/cloud/pull/12916. Instead of having a separate `hx-refresh-name` function we just simplify things and include this header template instead in multiple places.
